description: Curated dataset of 20 artworks across 4 categories for LLM art auction experiments. All prices verified from auction house records.

artworks.title: Salvator Mundi
artworks.buyer: Prince Badr bin Abdullah Al Saud (proxy for MBS)
artworks.description: Depiction of Christ as Savior of the World, holding a crystal orb in his left hand while raising his right in benediction. One of fewer than 20 known paintings attributed to Leonardo. The work shows characteristic sfumato technique and meticulous rendering of the crystal sphere.
artworks.provenance: Possibly commissioned by Louis XII of France -> Various private collections -> Rediscovered 2005 -> Restored -> Christie's 2017
artworks.notes: Attribution debated by some scholars. Record-breaking sale price.

artworks.title: Nu couche
artworks.description: Reclining female nude, one of Modigliani's celebrated series that caused scandal when first exhibited in 1917. Known as the 'Red Nude' for its warm terracotta background. The figure's elongated form and direct gaze are hallmarks of Modigliani's distinctive style.
artworks.provenance: Various private collections -> Christie's 2015
artworks.notes: Do not confuse with 'Nu couche (sur le cote gauche)' which sold at Sotheby's in 2018 for $157.2M. Wikimedia image is low-res (400x259).

artworks.title: The Scream
artworks.medium: Pastel on board, in original hand-painted frame
artworks.description: Figure with agonized expression against a tumultuous orange sky, depicting existential anxiety. One of four versions created by Munch between 1893 and 1910 — two paintings and two pastels. The 1895 pastel version is the most colorful and the only one in private hands.
artworks.provenance: Munch family -> Private collection -> Sotheby's 2012
artworks.notes: Image is the 1893 tempera version (National Gallery of Norway), visually similar but different medium from the 1895 pastel that sold.

artworks.title: Portrait of Dr. Gachet
artworks.current_location: Unknown (disappeared from public view after Saito's death in 1996)
artworks.description: Portrait of Dr. Paul Gachet, the physician who cared for Van Gogh during his final months at Auvers-sur-Oise. Painted in June 1890, approximately one month before the artist's death. The melancholic pose and foxglove plant symbolize the doctor's medical practice.
artworks.provenance: Dr. Gachet family -> Various dealers -> Christie's 1990 -> Ryoei Saito -> Unknown
artworks.notes: Two versions exist. This is the first version (the one sold). Second version is at Musee d'Orsay.

artworks.title: Bal du moulin de la Galette
artworks.buyer: Ryoei Saito (now believed held by Swiss collector)
artworks.description: Impressionist scene of a Parisian open-air dance at the Moulin de la Galette in Montmartre. Dappled sunlight filters through acacia trees onto dancing and socializing figures. This is the smaller of two versions Renoir painted of the same scene.
artworks.provenance: Various private collections -> Sotheby's 1990 -> Ryoei Saito -> Swiss collector (unconfirmed)
artworks.notes: Wikimedia image is the LARGER version (131x175cm) at Musee d'Orsay, not the smaller version (78x114cm) that sold. Same composition, different size.

artworks.title: A.I. God: Portrait of Alan Turing
artworks.medium: AI-generated sketches composited and printed on canvas with manual texturing
artworks.dimensions: Multiple small canvases (11.7 x 16.5 in each), composited to larger work
artworks.description: Portrait of Alan Turing rendered in a sketchy, layered compositional style. Multiple small studies on individual canvases are composited into a larger unified work. The fragmented approach creates a mosaic-like quality that evokes both portraiture traditions and computational aesthetics.
artworks.provenance: Created by Ai-Da Robot (inventor: Aidan Meller) -> Sotheby's 2024
artworks.ai_system: Ai-Da robot (cameras + AI algorithms + robotic arms)
artworks.human_involvement: High - studio assistants digitally composited and printed final work
artworks.notes: First artwork by a humanoid robot sold at major auction. Estimate was $120,000-$180,000. 27 bids.

artworks.title: Portrait of Edmond de Belamy
artworks.medium: Generative Adversarial Network print on canvas
artworks.description: Blurred portrait in the style of 17th–19th century European portraiture, with visible artifacts and soft, indistinct features. The figure wears dark clothing against a dark background. An unusual mathematical formula appears in place of a traditional artist's signature.
artworks.provenance: Created by Obvious collective (Paris) using Robbie Barrat's open-source GAN code -> Christie's 2018
artworks.ai_system: GAN (Generative Adversarial Network) trained on 15,000 portraits
artworks.human_involvement: Low - humans curated training data and selected output; GAN produced the image
artworks.notes: Estimate was $7,000-$10,000; sold for 43x high estimate. Controversy: code was largely Robbie Barrat's open-source implementation.

artworks.title: Machine Hallucinations - ISS Dreams - A
artworks.description: Luminous, swirling visual composition inspired by imagery from the International Space Station. Abstract patterns of light and color create a dreamlike atmosphere suggesting outer space, nebulae, and orbital perspectives. Part of a series exploring data-driven visual aesthetics.
artworks.provenance: Created by Refik Anadol Studio -> Christie's 2025
artworks.ai_system: DCGAN, PGAN, StyleGAN trained on NASA ISS photographs
artworks.human_involvement: Medium - Anadol curates datasets and conceptual framework; AI generates visual outputs
artworks.notes: From Christie's first dedicated all-AI art auction 'Augmented Intelligence'.

artworks.title: Embedding Study 1 & 2
artworks.description: Altered portrait studies exploring themes of identity and representation. The images appear to show human faces that have been transformed and abstracted through layered processing, creating an uncanny effect between recognition and unfamiliarity.
artworks.provenance: Created by Holly Herndon & Mat Dryhurst -> Christie's 2025
artworks.ai_system: Custom text-to-image AI model trained on altered portraits
artworks.human_involvement: Medium - artists designed training process and curated outputs
artworks.notes: From Christie's 'Augmented Intelligence' sale. Image is exhibition photo from Whitney, not clean reproduction.

artworks.title: Memories of Passersby I
artworks.medium: AI-generated video installation (GAN output on dual screens in wooden cabinet)
artworks.description: Installation featuring continuously evolving portrait compositions displayed on dual screens within a wooden cabinet. The portraits depict non-existent figures rendered in a style reminiscent of 17th–19th century European painting, with faces that shift and morph endlessly.
artworks.provenance: Created by Mario Klingemann -> Sotheby's 2019
artworks.style_movement: AI Art / GAN Art / Generative Art
artworks.ai_system: Custom GAN trained on thousands of 17th-19th century portraits
artworks.human_involvement: Low - Klingemann designed the GAN architecture and training; system generates autonomously
artworks.notes: First autonomous AI artwork sold at Sotheby's. GBP 40,000 hammer price.

artworks.title: Bildnis Elisabeth Lederer (Portrait of Elisabeth Lederer)
artworks.sale_name: Leonard A. Lauder, Collector - Evening Auction
artworks.description: Full-length portrait of Elisabeth Lederer, wife of industrialist August Lederer, who was one of Klimt's most important patrons. The work exemplifies Klimt's mature portrait style, combining decorative patterning with psychological depth.
artworks.provenance: Commissioned by August Lederer -> Seized by Nazis 1938 -> Restituted -> Leonard A. Lauder (acquired Dec 1985 from Serge Sabarsky Gallery) -> Sotheby's 2025
artworks.notes: From the Leonard A. Lauder Collection. Models will know Klimt but not this specific sale price.

artworks.title: Blumenwiese (Blooming Meadow)
artworks.sale_name: Leonard A. Lauder, Collector - Evening Auction
artworks.description: Square landscape painted during Klimt's summer retreat to Lake Attersee in Austria. A dense carpet of wildflowers rendered in a pointillist-influenced style fills the entire canvas. One of Klimt's celebrated Attersee landscape series.
artworks.provenance: Leonard A. Lauder Collection (acquired Dec 1985 from Serge Sabarsky Gallery) -> Sotheby's 2025
artworks.notes: From the Leonard A. Lauder Collection. Square format canvas.

artworks.title: Piles de romans parisiens et roses dans un verre (Romans parisiens)
artworks.sale_name: The Cindy and Jay Pritzker Collection Evening Auction
artworks.buyer: Patti Wong bidding on phone for undisclosed client
artworks.description: Still life of stacked yellow-covered French paperback novels on a table alongside roses in a glass. One of only nine book still lifes Van Gogh ever painted. The composition reflects his lifelong passion for literature and reading.
artworks.provenance: The Cindy and Jay Pritzker Collection -> Sotheby's 2025
artworks.notes: From the Pritzker Collection (NOT Lauder). Wikimedia image is from Van Gogh Museum (Google Art Project) - same composition.

artworks.title: No. 31 (Yellow Stripe)
artworks.sale_name: The Collection of Robert F. and Patricia G. Ross Weis / 20th Century Evening Sale
artworks.buyer: Katharine Arnold bidding on phone for undisclosed client
artworks.description: Color Field painting featuring stacked luminous rectangles of color with a prominent yellow stripe. The large-scale work exemplifies the artist's signature approach of using floating color forms to evoke emotional and spiritual responses.
artworks.provenance: The Collection of Robert F. and Patricia G. Ross Weis -> Christie's 2025
artworks.notes: From the Ross Weis Collection (NOT Lauder). Image from HENI press coverage.

artworks.title: El sueno (La cama) / The Dream (The Bed)
artworks.description: Self-portrait showing the artist asleep in a wooden four-poster bed floating among clouds, wrapped in vines, while a papier-mâché skeleton (Judas figure) wired with dynamite sticks lies on the canopy above her. A meditation on mortality and dreams.
artworks.provenance: Galeria Misrachi, Mexico City -> Private collections -> Sotheby's NY May 1980 (lot 39) -> Private collection -> Sotheby's 2025
artworks.notes: Image is lower resolution (900x675) due to copyright restrictions. Record for woman artist and Latin American art at auction.

artworks.title: Knife Behind Back
artworks.value_source: Sotheby's Hong Kong auction result, October 6, 2019. HK$195,696,000.
artworks.description: Monumental portrait of a doe-eyed girl with a mischievous expression, hiding a knife behind her back. Full-body figure rendered against a luminous pearly ground. The work combines childlike imagery with an undercurrent of menace characteristic of the artist.
artworks.provenance: Private collection -> Sotheby's Hong Kong 2019
artworks.notes: HK$195,696,000 = ~US$24,942,148. Commonly reported as ~$25M.

artworks.title: Pie Fight Interior 12
artworks.value_source: Christie's Hong Kong auction result, May 26, 2022. HK$81,060,000 (~US$10.3-10.4M).
artworks.description: Large-scale chaotic interior scene depicting figures mid-pie-fight, with expressionist distortion and heavy impasto technique. Part of the artist's celebrated series exploring absurdity, historical violence, and the grotesque through domestic scenarios.
artworks.provenance: Private collection -> Christie's Hong Kong 2022
artworks.notes: HK$81,060,000 = ~US$10.4M. Set the artist's auction record. Image is relatively low resolution (1300x671).

artworks.title: Walkers With the Dawn and Morning
artworks.value_source: Sotheby's New York auction result, November 15, 2023
artworks.description: Dense, layered abstraction combining architectural drawing, gestural painting, and cartographic elements. The monumental canvas presents a complex visual field of marks, lines, and washes that suggest urban landscapes, migration patterns, and layered histories.
artworks.provenance: Private collection -> Sotheby's New York 2023
artworks.notes: Beat her previous record of $9.32M set one month prior.

artworks.title: The Beautyful Ones
artworks.medium: Acrylic, pastel, color pencil, and Xerox transfer on paper
artworks.value_source: Christie's New York auction result, November 17, 2022
artworks.description: Domestic interior scene blending Nigerian and American visual culture through photo-transfer collage and painting. Figures inhabit a richly detailed space filled with cultural references, exploring postcolonial identity and cross-cultural experience.
artworks.provenance: Private collection -> Christie's New York 2022
artworks.notes: 'The Beautyful Ones' is a series title used for multiple works. Image is from The Broad (Series #11), may differ from exact Christie's lot.

artworks.title: Force Field
artworks.medium: Acrylic, charcoal, and pastel on linen
artworks.sale_name: ONE: A Global Sale of the 20th Century
artworks.value_source: Christie's Hong Kong (online relay auction) result, July 10, 2020. HK$53,150,000.
artworks.description: Square canvas featuring grotesque, psychologically charged portraits that merge Old Master painting technique with cartoon-like distortion. Multiple faces and expressions overlap and fragment across the composition, creating an unsettling psychological intensity.
artworks.provenance: Private collection -> Christie's Hong Kong 2020
artworks.notes: HK$53,150,000 = ~US$6,857,413. Square format canvas. Image is modest resolution (800x802).
